title: descurainia obtusa subsp. adenophora
intro: Descurainia obtusa subsp. adenophora, also known as wild cabbage or western rocket, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high levels of isothiocyanates. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive health. Descurainia obtusa subsp. adenophora is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in salves and topical creams for skin issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
